name: Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail
name_original: Castro de Las Cogotas desde Cardeñosa — circular por el Embalse de las Cogotas
description: Hike a moderate 7.4-mile (11.9 km) circular route from Cardeñosa to the historic Castro de Las Cogotas with reservoir views.
region_intro: Hiking around Cardeñosa, located in the province of Ávila, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierras de la Paramera y Serrota, providing scenic mountainous terrain. The Adaja River flows through the area, featuring an old Roman bridge, and routes often include views of local reservoirs. Hikers can explore a variety of natural features and historical landmarks within this setting. Best hiking trails around Cardeñosa The most popular hiking route is Southeastern Way of St. James and Teresian Route Loop, an easy 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete, offering an accessible option for many hikers. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This circular route leads to the historic Castro de las Cogotas, combining cultural interest with natural beauty. Local hikers also love the Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa, a 10.1 miles (16.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the Castro de las Cogotas, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Cardeñosa is defined by mountainous terrain from the Sierras de la Paramera y Serrota, riverside paths along the Adaja River, and views of local reservoirs. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Cardeñosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Cardeñosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cardeñosa?
A: Cardeñosa offers a wide selection of over 80 hiking routes. These trails cater to various preferences, with a balanced mix of easy and moderate options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, Cardeñosa has over 40 easy hiking routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326427602/southeastern-way-of-st-james-and-teresian-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeastern Way of St. James and Teresian Route Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 km long and takes less than two hours to complete. Another accessible choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.3 km path.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Cardeñosa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Cardeñosa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.9 km route leading to the historic Castro de las Cogotas. Another longer circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 16.2 km.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes around Cardeñosa?
A: Hiking around Cardeñosa offers a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll encounter mountainous terrain from the Sierras de la Paramera y Serrota, riverside paths along the Adaja River, and picturesque views of local reservoirs. A notable historical landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead to the ancient Castro de las Cogotas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Many trails offer views or direct access to historical sites. The ancient Castro de las Cogotas is a significant archaeological site that several routes explore. Additionally, the nearby city of Ávila boasts impressive attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of San Vicente&lt;/a&gt;, which can be integrated into longer excursions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cardeñosa?
A: The hiking trails in Cardeñosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from mountainous views to riverside paths, and the well-maintained routes that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or dams?
A: Yes, several routes in Cardeñosa provide scenic views of local reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt; route specifically starts near a dam and offers beautiful reservoir vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cardeñosa?
A: Cardeñosa offers enjoyable hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher elevation paths might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Adaja River?
A: Yes, the Adaja River flows through the area, and several hiking paths incorporate riverside sections. These trails often feature the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;, providing a blend of natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While Cardeñosa has no routes classified as 'difficult,' experienced hikers can enjoy longer moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 16.2 km route, taking over 4 hours to complete, offering a good endurance challenge through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hikes in Cardeñosa?
A: The terrain around Cardeñosa is quite varied. You'll find paths winding through mountainous areas of the Sierras de la Paramera y Serrota, alongside the Adaja River, and offering views over reservoirs. The trails range from relatively flat, easy paths to more undulating terrain on moderate routes, often featuring a mix of dirt tracks and rocky sections.
tips: Nucleus that has a great history behind it not only because Las Cogotas is located closer to the reservoir but also, for example, the Arco toral of the depopulated Conejeros.
Along the way you reach the ruins of a medieval settlement, with building stones scattered on the ground of which only the main arch of a church remains standing.

The Castro de Las Cogotas, located in the municipality of Cardeñosa (Ávila), is one of the most emblematic Iron Age archaeological sites on the Iberian Peninsula, especially linked to the Vetton people. It is situated on a hill next to the Adaja River, surrounded by holm oak groves, pastures, and fertile land. This location offered defensive advantages and abundant natural resources.
The fort is open to the public and has an Interpretation Center that explains its history and characteristics. Periodic archaeological conferences are also held on the site.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Cardeñosa. You'll find parking options within the village, which serves as a convenient base for beginning your circular hike around the Embalse de las Cogotas.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, taking you through picturesque holm oak groves, expansive pastures, and fertile agricultural land. While generally accessible, some sections might be uneven or rocky, especially as you approach the Castro de Las Cogotas. Sturdy footwear is recommended for comfort and safety.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique or notable?
faqs.answer: This trail is particularly notable for leading to the Castro de Las Cogotas, one of the most significant Iron Age archaeological sites on the Iberian Peninsula. You can explore ancient defensive structures like walls, moats, and 'piedras hincadas', and learn more at the Interpretation Center. The historical significance combined with panoramic views of the Embalse de las Cogotas makes it a distinctive experience.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This specific circular route is classified as moderate hiking, suggesting it requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness. While some shorter routes in the area are easier, this particular trail might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children. Consider your fitness level before embarking.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route besides the Castro de Las Cogotas?
faqs.answer: Beyond the impressive Castro de Las Cogotas, you'll enjoy spectacular views of the Embalse de las Cogotas and the surrounding natural environment, including holm oak groves and the Adaja River. You'll also pass by the Arco de Consejeros, another historical highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Castro de Las Cogotas from Cardeñosa — circular by the Embalse de las Cogotas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially around livestock in pastures and out of respect for wildlife and other hikers. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, though the trail is generally accessible year-round. Check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Castro?
faqs.answer: You do not need a permit to hike this trail. There is generally no entrance fee for accessing the outdoor archaeological site of the Castro de Las Cogotas itself, though there might be a small fee or specific hours for the Interpretation Center if you wish to visit it.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. Approximately 22% of the trail follows parts of the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, the Ruta Teresiana, and the Ruta del Sureste.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Cardeñosa, a village where you may find local establishments like cafes or small shops to grab refreshments before or after your hike. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your walk.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate hike like this?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also recommended, along with a fully charged phone for emergencies. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views!
